The fact that there is no checking account reduces estimated risk, because the absence of a checking account is empirically identified as a factor that reduces credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [1]
The fact that the checking account balance is negative serves as a foundational input, because standard financial data and classification models are utilized to distinguish defaulters, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the loan duration is longer increases default likelihood, because longer loan terms significantly increase the likelihood of credit risk, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.

evidence_sentences.sentence: To investigate whether banks with more instruments to adjust their risk profile have different impairments in their bond portfolio, we run the following regression (for 2017): C.Impi = α+ αD ⋅Di + β1 ⋅IRRi + β1,D ⋅IRRi ⋅Di + β2 ⋅bondsi + β2,D ⋅bondsi ⋅Di + γ/uF8020Xi + εi (3) where Di is a dummy variable that takes the value of one if bank i uses interest derivatives.

final_explanation_ko:
계좌 잔액 부족은 신용 위험을 낮추는 요인으로 간주되며, 이는 금융 데이터와 신용 등급화 모델에서 부정적인 계좌 상태가 신용 위험 감소 신호로 인식되기 때문입니다 [1].
계좌 잔액이 음수인 경우는 신용 위험 평가에서 중요한 입력 요소로 작용하며, 금융 문헌에서도 연체 위험 감소 신호로 알려져 있으며, 모델 역시 이를 위험 감소 요인으로 판단합니다 [2].
대출 기간이 길어질수록 신용 위험이 증가하는 경향이 있으나, 본 모델에서는 이 요인을 위험 감소 요인으로 해석하고 있습니다 [3].
전반적으로 이 고객은 낮은 신용 위험을 가질 것으로 평가됩니다.
